Name: Preparation of Internal Memoranda on behalf of Directorate/Management
Actvities: Preparation of internal memoranda on behalf ofDirectorate/Management
Outcome: Memoranda availiable/disseminated
Details: • Management’s approval • Draft from surbodinate• Presence in DCP approved activity plan/Management’s directives (Statutory Meeting deliverables), etc. • Reference documents when necessary • Outcome of HODs meeting/ Division meetings • Request for response from Management • Management’s instructions • Request from ClientDirectorate/DCP staff

Name: Receipt of Incoming Correspondences and Requests
Outcome: DCP actions on requests
Details: • Verification of addressee • Verification of required supporting documents(Released PR number,completed meeting request form)

Name: Review of Note Verbales and Issuance of Numbers
Actvities: Review of Note Verbale and issuance of numbers
Outcome: Note Verbale ready for initialing and stamping
Details: • Evidence of duly approved activity • Evidence of correctly drafted Note Verbale, free of errors

Name: Supervision of Preparations for Statutory Meetings and other Official Events
Actvities: Supervision of preparations for Statutory Meetings and  other official events
Outcome: Event is held successfully
Details: • Receipt of approved dates from Management, list and level of representation of invited guests, ECOWAS order of precedence • Approved budget, approved calendar of events, approved list of participants, approved standard rates, accurate translation of all document and meeting titles, details  for budgets from support directorates • Updated and accurate directory of members of statutuory bodies in Member States

Name: Preparations for Statutory Meetings/Official Events
Outcome: • Meeting holds successfully • Approved meeting budget, approved composition of delegation, approved documentation for release, all meeting facilities availiable • Cover memos to GA, Comm and ITS, SG for and content and support meeting logistics
Details: • Approval/indication of  approved dates and duration form management • Request for agenda items from all Community Institutions • Meeting documents • Official Note Verbale • Liaison with host member state • Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on hosting of ECOWAS meetings • Outcome of consultations with HODs and support directorates

Name: Preparation and Review of Contributions to Reports
Actvities: Preparation and review of contributions to reports
Outcome: DCP contribution to report ready for clearance by Management/dispatch to directorate collating final report
Details: • Official instruction from president/CGAC • Elements from HODs where necessary (translation log, language schedule, consular activities, participation in HR activities, details of cooperation with external partners)

Name: Preparation of Concept Notes/Terms of Reference
Actvities: Preparation of concept notes/terms of reference
Outcome: Approval for requested service/activity is obtained
Details: • Approved activity plan • Needs within Directorate as identified by supervising officers • Instruction from Management for recruitment of provider/consultant for schedule of duty for which DCP is responsible

Name: Preparation of Internal Meetings
Outcome: Succcessful and effective meeting is held
Details: • Matters arising within Directorate • Implementation of Management’s instruction

Name: Document Upload, Population and Management of ECODOCS for Paperless Meeting
Actvities: Document upload, population and management of ECODOCS for paperless meeting
Outcome: • All offically released electronic documents availiable to all accredited meeting participants • Reduction in use of paper • Increased familiarity with use of technology in meeting logistics
Details: • Approved meeting agenda • Accurate status of documents • Accurate status of translation • Receipt of documents from Management • Offical clearance to upload and release received from President

Name: Assessing Interpretation Service Request
Outcome: The interpretation service request meeting requirements is validated
Details: Needs: • Memo minuted by DCP • Required information • Supporting documents (budget adequacy, purchase requisition (PR) number, working documents, meeting venue, working languages and number of participants)

Name: Processing the Validated Interpretation Service Request and Mounting Team(s)
Actvities: Processing the validated interpretation service request and mounting team(s)
Outcome: Interpretation service request is processed and a team(s) is mounted and properly weighted to service the assigned meeting
Details: • Roster of service providers (SIE and language professionals)/available staff/accurate language schedule • Proposed weighted team of interpreters • Proposed SIE provider • Endorsed SIE proforma invoices • Freelancers’ contracts • Link for virtual meetings • Indication of meeting venue • Working documents • PR number

Name: Deploying Interpreters in a Physical Meeting
Actvities: Deploying interpreters in a physical meeting
Outcome: Participants communicate and understand each other expressing themselves in any agreed working languages
Details: Needs: • Language schedule • Roster (freelancers and SIE providers) • Freelancers’ contracts • Ticket requests • TVM completed by all staff interpreters for meetings outside the duty station • Tickets appropriately issued for interpreters • SIE provider’s proforma invoice • Dedicated hostesses

Name: Deployment of Interpreters’ Team in Virtual Meetings
Actvities: Deployment of interpreters’ team in virtual meetings
Outcome: Participants communicate and understand each other expressing themselves in any agreed working languages in a virtual setting
Details: Needs: • Language schedule • Freelancers’ contracts • Contact details of interpreters • Hosts/co-hosts and ITS • Link • Interpreters’ co-host roster • Interpreters’ slots • Output disclaimer

Name: Deployment of Interpreters’ Team in Hybrid Meetings
Actvities: Deployment of interpreters’ team in hybrid meetings
Outcome: Participants communicate and understand each other expressing themselves in any agreed working languages in a hybrid setting
Details: Needs: • Mandatory presence of ITS • Interface and cabling • TVM completed by all staff interpreters • Working documents • Link • Roster (freelancers and SIE providers) • Tickets appropriately issued for interpreters • Dedicated hostesses • Routers • Language schedule • Freelancers’ contracts • Output disclaimer

Name: Inspection
Outcome: Physical and technical requirements are met
Details: Needs: • Name and contact details of SIE provider • SIE components and interpretation platform Interface on site mounted and functional • Presence of the technician and Conference Division • Presence of LC/Team Leader and 2 interpreters (staff) • Availability of power and backup power (UPS) • Face time between LC/Team Leader and technician • Designated transportation for interpreters

Name: Sound Check For Physical Meetings
Outcome: Sound parameters are good
Details: Needs: • Presence of interpreters and technician(s) • SIE

Name: Hybrid Meetings Sound Check
Outcome: Sound parameters are good
Details: Needs: • Presence of a sufficient number of technicians ordinary meeting:  • - 1 to 50 participants: 1 technician  • - 50 and above: 2 technicians  •  statutory meetings: 3 technicians • Deploy accordingly to cover spillover rooms as well • Presence of interpreters • SIE • Interpetation platform & accessories • Link

Name: Virtual meetings sound check
Outcome: Input and ouput sound are clear
Details: Needs: • Link • Presence of meeting host • Interpreters’ co-host and interpretersds: • Presence of interpreters and ITS • Link • Interpetation platform & accessories

Name: Paying Freelance Interpreters and SIE Provider
Actvities: Paying freelance interpreters and SIE provider
Outcome: Freelance interpreters and SIE providers are paid their entitlements for services rendered
Details: Needs: • Relevant payment information and documents (signed contracts and supporting documents, endorsed SIE proforma invoice among others)

Name: Receiving Feedback
Outcome: Feedback is received from the client department and participants to point out shortcomings and improve service delivery
Details: Needs: • Feedback forms • Survey monkey

Details: • Approved memo • Letter of invitation • Evidence of submission of online application form • Receipt of payment of visa fee • Original copy of national passport • Health insurance certificate • Two passport photographs • Any other requirement on the Consulate website

Name: Accreditation of Heads of Mission to ECOWAS, Presentation of Letter of Credence
Actvities: Accreditation of Heads of Mission to ECOWAS/Presentation of Letter of Credence
Outcome: Heads of Mission are duly accredited to the Commission
Details: • Memo from DCP notifying the event, stating date, time and venue, with detailed list of countries and heads of mission who by time of accreditation at the Commission should already be accredited to the Federal Republic of Nigeria • Venue • Notice to General Administration and Conference Division for support • Prepared venue with Diplomatic paraphenalia and insignia • Light refreshment

Actvities: Diplomatic Cocktail, Reception of the ECOWAS President

Details: • Flight plan • Letter of appointment • Telephone and email contact of the new staff • Letter of introduction to accommodation facility • Transportation

Name: Customs Duties’ Waivers
Outcome: Successful clearing of items belonging to the Commission or staff at port of entry
Details: • 3 original bills of lading (destination address reads ECOWAS Commission) • Purchase receipt • Custom clearance paper form CC3 /CC1 • Car particulars, • Data page of entry passport bearing entry stamp of 1st arrival • Data page of laissez-passer • Packing list
